text: V1 scope is exactly the 12 tools named in spec/version-1.md section 4.
reason: Spec is unambiguous; tool surface must match exactly for downstream orchestration.
impact: Public tool names, input/output schemas, and tool count are locked.

text: Rust plus Tree-sitter as the parser layer.
reason: Recommended by the spec; matches agent-server norms; Tree-sitter is the de facto standard for syntax trees across all 5 V1 languages.
impact: Lock tree-sitter and language crate versions in Cargo.toml. parser/registry.rs is the single extension point for future languages.

reason: Spec section 7. Multi-root is a V5 concern.
impact: WORKSPACE_PATH env var or CWD. All path resolution goes through safety::paths.

text: Read-only server, no caches in V1.
reason: Spec sections 8.2 and 5. Caching is a V5 concern.
impact: Each request that needs a parse re-parses. Tests may use a per-test cache.

reason: Spec section 9. Aligns with LSP contract for future orchestration.
impact: Conversion helpers in parser/positions.rs. Tests for non-BMP characters.

text: Bounded output is mandatory, not optional.
reason: Spec section 8.3. AST output can be huge; unbounded responses can OOM the client.
impact: Every list- and text-returning tool has a maxXxx parameter and a truncated field.

text: Errors are structured JSON, never prose.
reason: Spec sections 12 and 28. Tools are consumed by agents, not humans; free-text errors are unparseable.
impact: A single error-shape helper. All tools funnel through shared::errors.

reason: The spec lists mcp/, config/, safety/, parser/, languages/, extractors/, tools/, shared/, utils/. This layout cleanly separates transport, parsing, and tool handlers.
impact: File layout is locked. extractors/ is the primary place where language-specific code lives; tools/ is one thin file per MCP tool.

text: The 12 development milestones become the phase plan.
reason: Spec section 33 enumerates 12 milestones, each a small, testable, committable unit. The phasing guide recommends phase-per-milestone for projects that already come with a clear, sequential plan.
impact: 12 phases, mapped 1:1 to milestones, with phases 1-4 (skeleton, safety, parsers, basic parsing) being high-risk and gates to the rest.

reason: Spec section 36. V1 design must keep extension points open, but the implementation must not pre-build V2 capabilities.
alternatives: Build a plugin layer in V1
impact: No premature abstraction. parser/registry.rs is extensible by addition, not by configuration.

text: Architectural lint tests enforce no-write and no-LSP.
reason: Constraints c-1 and c-2 are the most important invariants in the contract. Manual review is error-prone.
impact: Two tests in tests/architecture/. Both ship in phase 2 so the invariant is protected from day one.

text: The 4 chunking strategies ship in one phase.
reason: They share the same chunk-iteration core; splitting them across phases adds coordination cost without benefit.
impact: Milestone 10 ships all four strategies together, with per-strategy unit tests and a shared integration test.
